Description: This elective course covers modern web technologies and full-stack development foundations. Students explore client-side and server-side scripting, including HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and backend frameworks. The curriculum emphasizes designing, building, and deploying dynamic, interactive, and responsive web applications.

This elective course introduces fundamental concepts of probability theory and mathematical statistics. Students learn about random variables, probability distributions, independence, expectation, variance, and the central limit theorem. The course focuses on analyzing random phenomena and developing statistical thinking for data evaluation.

This introductory course covers fundamental concepts of linear algebra and analytic geometry. Students explore matrices, determinants, vector spaces, linear transformations, and systems of linear equations. The curriculum focuses on developing abstract reasoning, mathematical proofs, and problem-solving skills essential for advanced sciences.

This foundational course introduces core concepts of computer science and structured programming using a high-level language. It covers variables, data types, control flow structures, functions, arrays, and basic algorithms for searching and sorting. Students develop practical skills in writing, debugging, and analyzing efficient code to solve computational problems.
